subject: Statistics
title: Randomized Controlled Trials
description: A 60-second lesson on Randomized Controlled Trials.

hook.text: In 1948, the first modern RCT tested streptomycin for tuberculosis. It changed medicine forever.

buildup.text: Patients were randomly assigned to treatment or control. Randomization ensures groups are comparable.

discovery.text: Randomization balances both known and unknown factors. Even variables you didn't think of get balanced.

twist.text: Without randomization, sicker patients might get the drug. The treatment looks worse because the groups differ.

climax.text: The RCT is called the gold standard of evidence. It's the most reliable way to test if a treatment works.

punchline.text: Random assignment removes bias. That's the gold standard.

quiz.question: Why is randomization important in clinical trials?
options: It balances known and unknown factors between groups, ensuring a fair comparison
It makes the trial faster to complete
It guarantees the drug will work
